title: Data Ownership and Source-of-Truth Confusion
summary: Multiple services or modules read and write the same data without a clear owner.
inspection_goal: Identify ambiguous data control paths and competing writers around the same entities or stores.
primary_signals: Multiple independent writers to the same store or entity path
Read and write paths crossing many ownership boundaries
Several components acting as effective sources of truth
Synchronization or replication edges with unclear ownership
inspection_questions: Which data stores or entities have many writers?
Is there a clear owner for each important data domain?
Do readers and writers align with intended boundaries?
Where might duplicate or stale state emerge?
evidence_to_collect: Writer counts per store or entity node
recommendation_patterns: Assign clear ownership to each data domain
Use explicit synchronization or event contracts where duplication is intentional
Separate read models from write ownership where needed

severity_guidance.high: Critical data domains have multiple uncontrolled writers and unclear ownership.
severity_guidance.medium: Ownership is mostly clear but with visible overlap in some domains.
severity_guidance.low: Data ownership is explicit with only minor ambiguity.
